message:
fix(fcp-core/capability): remove legacy GRANTS/OPERATIONS fallback (br-8n0rm.6)

Deletes the 28-line `else` branch at capability.rs:1845-1874 that
fell through to parsing `fcp2_claims::OPERATIONS` when
`fcp2_claims::GRANTS` was absent. The verifier now requires the
canonical GRANTS shape uniformly.

Safe to remove because 8n0rm.8 made CapabilityTokenBuilder.sign()
auto-synthesize GRANTS from (capability_id + operations) for every
legacy issuer. Tokens minted by the current builder always carry
GRANTS; tokens reaching the verifier without GRANTS are malformed
(hand-crafted with .custom(...) in a bypass pattern, or from a
pre-8n0rm.8 issuer that needs upgrading).

Added regression test:
- verifier_rejects_legacy_operations_only_token: hand-crafts a
  CwtClaims with legacy OPERATIONS but NO GRANTS (bypassing the
  auto-synthesis in .sign()), signs it, and asserts the verifier
  returns MissingField("caps"). Proves the fallback is gone.

Verified:
- 260 fcp-core capability tests green
- 5 fcp-conformance cross-crate tests green
- 3 fcp-host gateway-handoff E2E tests green
- No regressions in the broader test surface

Closes br-8n0rm.6. The 8n0rm epic's single-source-of-truth goal is
now fully in place: one typed AuthClaims schema in fcp-auth-schema,
one canonical GRANTS shape, no drift paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
